start.line: Welcome to Brill's. Returns are a privilege, not a right, and I am the privilege.

options.text: I'd like to return a jacket.
What exactly is your return policy?
Do you validate feelings? Parking? Anything?

jacket.line: A jacket. With a receipt, with the tags on, with — and I cannot stress this enough — a good attitude. Show me the item and the paper, in that order, and we'll see.

options.text: The item and the paper. Understood.
What if I only have one of the two?

howto.line: Hand me the jacket while you're holding the receipt. That's the whole rite. I don't make the rules — I merely enforce them with my entire personality.

options.text: The jacket, with the receipt. Got it.

onlyone.line: One of two is zero of two. A jacket without a receipt is a story. A receipt without a jacket is a wish. I trade in neither. Bring me the matched set.

policy.line: RETURNS ACCEPTED — with receipt, with tags, with a good attitude, and within a window I define after you've waited. The attitude clause is new. I added it. In pen. After a man named Kessler.

options.text: You added a clause about ATTITUDE?

attitude.line: The policy is the last honest thing in this store, sir. Everything else is on sale. The policy is not on sale. The policy is load-bearing.

validate.line: I validate returns. That's it. That's the list. Your feelings can take the escalator to Housewares like everyone else's.
